adds.rationale: Largest Western uranium producer benefits directly from the Aug-2022 Inflation Reduction Act's nuclear tax credits and HALEU funding, plus European energy-crisis-driven reconsideration of nuclear restarts; diversifies the nuclear bucket beyond the URA miner-basket with a single-name pure-play.

news_evidence.summary: Inflation Reduction Act signed Aug 16, 2022 allocates ~$10B+ to nuclear, including a zero-emission nuclear PTC and $700M for HALEU fuel supply chain - first major US federal policy tailwind for uranium demand in decades.
news_evidence.source: Morgan Lewis / U.S. Department of Energy

news_evidence.summary: Cameco restarted production at McArthur River and Key Lake in 2022 as uranium spot prices recovered, signaling supply-side confidence that the post-Fukushima glut is working off.

Germany delayed shutdown of remaining nuclear plants past end-2022 and Japan signaled restart momentum for its idled fleet, both responses to Russia gas-supply shock - structural demand shift for uranium.

adds.rationale: Broader, more industrial-automation-weighted complement to BOTZ at a moment when factory-automation and autonomous-systems subsectors are leading the post-summer recovery; cheap entry into a profile-named next wave while it is still being sold off with the rest of growth.

news_evidence.summary: ROBO Global Robotics & Automation Index ETF posted ~+9% in Q3 2022, led by Manufacturing & Industrial Automation +21.1% (Teradyne, Celestica, Hon Hai) and Autonomous Systems +24.8% (Joby, Tesla, XPeng) - signs the industrial automation cycle is bottoming.

ROBO index added Coherent (COHR), Infineon (IFNNY), and UBTECH this quarter, deepening exposure to humanoid/service-robot and power-electronics names that the user's thesis on the next robotics wave would want.

adds.rationale: First and most liquid pure-play quantum-computing equity, hitting concrete commercial and technical milestones in Q3 2022 - exactly the buildup-phase signature the profile says to buy early and cheap while the wave is years from peak enthusiasm.

news_evidence.summary: IonQ Q2 2022 revenue $2.6M (vs. $233K prior-year) and signed a $13.4M contract with U.S. Air Force Research Lab - first material government commercial validation for trapped-ion quantum computing.
IonQ achieved 25 algorithmic qubits on IonQ Aria, a 4x improvement and an industry record on the algorithmic-qubit benchmark - meaningful technical progress on the company's stated 2022 roadmap.
IonQ announced a hybrid quantum-classical platform partnership with Dell using Dell PowerEdge servers - distribution-channel signal that classical-hardware vendors are starting to bundle quantum.

rationale_overall: Watchlist grows from 8 to 11 (one slot kept open) by adding one ticker each in three under-represented next-wave buckets named in the profile: nuclear (CCJ alongside URA), robotics (ROBO alongside BOTZ), and quantum (IONQ, first entry). No removes: the current AI trio (AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL) and core market sleeves (SPY/AGG) are intact theses, and a ride-the-wave/trim-before-crest stance does not warrant trimming AI during a deep bear-market drawdown when valuations have already compressed. The net effect is a clean tilt from a still-AI-heavy starter list toward the user's stated next-wave portfolio shape, taken at a moment when growth and thematic ETFs are being sold off broadly.
